_comment.1: SOUNDCARD delay via a continuous 20 Hz drive (play_tone 20 1 is known to
_comment.2: oscillate the beam). test_source sets ALL vector elements equal, so it
_comment.3: drives BOTH soundcard channels at once (like play_tone) -> whichever is
_comment.4: wired to y-fine moves the beam. We record the DRIVE (command, after
_comment.5: test_source) and the RESPONSE (CoM [y,x], before test_source) every
_comment.6: frame; the delay is the time lag between them (cross-correlation),
_comment.7: measured offline by contrib/analyze_20hz.py. This is the FULL loop delay
_comment.9: sensing delay to isolate the actuator. Caveat: AC-coupling adds a small
_comment.10: phase lead at 20 Hz, so the lag slightly UNDER-estimates the true delay.
_comment.12: at the 3788 Hz camera loop (cross-correlation is robust to small rate
_comment.13: error anyway). ROI 272,392 = current beam centre.

pipeline._note: RESPONSE: CoM [y,x] every frame (element 0 = y = the driven axis).
20 Hz sine into BOTH command elements (test_source sets all equal). amplitude 1.0 = full-scale soundcard drive (matches play_tone 20 1). frequency 0.033174 rad/iter = 20 Hz at 3788 Hz loop.
DRIVE: the command [y,x] every frame (both = the 20 Hz sine).
soundcard, low-latency threaded (0.083 ms queue). Both channels driven.
piplate parked: holds coarse + x-fine at 2 V bias (y-fine now on the soundcard).
